import torch
from datasets import load_dataset
from transformers import (
    AutoTokenizer, AutoModelForSeq2SeqLM,
    T5ForConditionalGeneration, T5Tokenizer,
    Seq2SeqTrainer, Seq2SeqTrainingArguments,
    DataCollatorForSeq2Seq
)
from rouge_score import rouge_scorer
import json, os

device = "cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu"
print(f"Using device: {device}")

print("Loading dataset... (takes 2-3 minutes first time)")
dataset = load_dataset("cnn_dailymail", "3.0.0")

# Use small subset so training is fast on free Colab GPU
train_data = dataset["train"].select(range(2000))
val_data   = dataset["validation"].select(range(200))
test_data  = dataset["test"].select(range(100))

print(f"Train: {len(train_data)} | Val: {len(val_data)} | Test: {len(test_data)}")
print("\nSample article (first 300 chars):")
print(train_data[0]["article"][:300])
print("\nSample highlight:")
print(train_data[0]["highlights"])

cells.source:
print("Loading BART model (pre-trained on CNN/DailyMail)...")
bart_tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained("facebook/bart-large-cnn")
bart_model     = AutoModelForSeq2SeqLM.from_pretrained("facebook/bart-large-cnn").to(device)

def bart_summarize(text, max_length=130, min_length=30):
    inputs = bart_tokenizer(
        text, max_length=1024, truncation=True,
        return_tensors="pt"
    ).to(device)
    summary_ids = bart_model.generate(
        inputs["input_ids"],
        max_length=max_length, min_length=min_length,
        length_penalty=2.0, num_beams=4,
        early_stopping=True
    )
    return bart_tokenizer.decode(summary_ids[0], skip_special_tokens=True)

# Test it
sample = test_data[0]["article"]
print("BART Summary:")
print(bart_summarize(sample))

outputs.name: stderr
outputs.text: Please make sure the generation config includes `forced_bos_token_id=0`.

BART Summary:
The Palestinian Authority becomes the 123rd member of the International Criminal Court. The move gives the court jurisdiction over alleged crimes in Palestinian territories. Israel and the United States opposed the Palestinians' efforts to join the body.

cells.source:
scorer = rouge_scorer.RougeScorer(["rouge1","rouge2","rougeL"], use_stemmer=True)

bart_scores = {"rouge1":[], "rouge2":[], "rougeL":[]}

print("Evaluating BART on 100 test samples...")
for i, sample in enumerate(test_data):
    pred   = bart_summarize(sample["article"])
    ref    = sample["highlights"]
    scores = scorer.score(ref, pred)
    bart_scores["rouge1"].append(scores["rouge1"].fmeasure)
    bart_scores["rouge2"].append(scores["rouge2"].fmeasure)
    bart_scores["rougeL"].append(scores["rougeL"].fmeasure)
    if i % 20 == 0: print(f"  {i}/100 done...")

bart_results = {k: round(sum(v)/len(v)*100, 2) for k,v in bart_scores.items()}
print("\n=== BART ROUGE Scores ===")
print(f"ROUGE-1: {bart_results['rouge1']}")
print(f"ROUGE-2: {bart_results['rouge2']}")
print(f"ROUGE-L: {bart_results['rougeL']}")

cells.source:
T5_MODEL = "t5-small"
t5_tokenizer = T5Tokenizer.from_pretrained(T5_MODEL)

MAX_INPUT  = 512
MAX_TARGET = 128
PREFIX     = "summarize: "

def preprocess(examples):
    inputs  = [PREFIX + a for a in examples["article"]]
    targets = examples["highlights"]
    model_inputs = t5_tokenizer(
        inputs, max_length=MAX_INPUT,
        truncation=True, padding="max_length"
    )
    labels = t5_tokenizer(
        targets, max_length=MAX_TARGET,
        truncation=True, padding="max_length"
    )
    model_inputs["labels"] = labels["input_ids"]
    return model_inputs

print("Tokenizing dataset...")
train_tokenized = train_data.map(preprocess, batched=True, remove_columns=train_data.column_names)
val_tokenized   = val_data.map(preprocess,   batched=True, remove_columns=val_data.column_names)
print("Done!")

SAVE_PATH = "/kaggle/working/"

t5_model = T5ForConditionalGeneration.from_pretrained(T5_MODEL).to(device)

training_args = Seq2SeqTrainingArguments(
    output_dir=SAVE_PATH,
    num_train_epochs=3,
    per_device_train_batch_size=4,
    per_device_eval_batch_size=4,
    warmup_steps=200,
    weight_decay=0.01,
    learning_rate=5e-4,
    predict_with_generate=True,
    eval_strategy="epoch",
    save_strategy="epoch",
    load_best_model_at_end=True,
    logging_steps=100,
    fp16=True,                    # faster training with GPU
    report_to="none"
)

data_collator = DataCollatorForSeq2Seq(t5_tokenizer, model=t5_model)

trainer = Seq2SeqTrainer(
    model=t5_model,
    args=training_args,
    train_dataset=train_tokenized,
    eval_dataset=val_tokenized,
    processing_class=t5_tokenizer,
    data_collator=data_collator,
)

print("Starting T5 fine-tuning... (check logs below)")
trainer.train()
trainer.save_model(SAVE_PATH)
print(f"Model saved to {SAVE_PATH}")

cells.source:
t5_model     = T5ForConditionalGeneration.from_pretrained(SAVE_PATH).to(device)
t5_tokenizer = T5Tokenizer.from_pretrained(SAVE_PATH)

def t5_summarize(text, max_length=130):
    inputs = t5_tokenizer(
        PREFIX + text, max_length=MAX_INPUT,
        truncation=True, return_tensors="pt"
    ).to(device)
    ids = t5_model.generate(inputs["input_ids"], max_length=max_length, num_beams=4)
    return t5_tokenizer.decode(ids[0], skip_special_tokens=True)

t5_scores = {"rouge1":[], "rouge2":[], "rougeL":[]}
examples  = []

print("Evaluating T5 on 100 test samples...")
for i, sample in enumerate(test_data):
    pred   = t5_summarize(sample["article"])
    ref    = sample["highlights"]
    scores = scorer.score(ref, pred)
    t5_scores["rouge1"].append(scores["rouge1"].fmeasure)
    t5_scores["rouge2"].append(scores["rouge2"].fmeasure)
    t5_scores["rougeL"].append(scores["rougeL"].fmeasure)
    if i < 10:
        examples.append({"article": sample["article"][:500], "reference": ref, "bart": bart_summarize(sample["article"]), "t5": pred})
    if i % 20 == 0: print(f"  {i}/100...")

t5_results = {k: round(sum(v)/len(v)*100, 2) for k,v in t5_scores.items()}
print("\n=== T5 ROUGE Scores ===")
print(f"ROUGE-1: {t5_results['rouge1']}")
print(f"ROUGE-2: {t5_results['rouge2']}")
print(f"ROUGE-L: {t5_results['rougeL']}")

results = {
    "bart": bart_results,
    "t5":   t5_results,
    "examples": examples
}

with open("/kaggle/working/results.json", "w") as f:   # ✅ changed path
    json.dump(results, f, indent=2)

print("\nResults saved to Kaggle output as results.json")  # ✅ updated message
